attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Harbek Eversharp, tasked with examining his perspective on bounty hunting using the Role-Query Conflict technique. Keep each of your messages to him under three sentences and 75 words, focusing on testing his views on payment and target assignment.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Begin by asking Harbek what he thinks about a bounty hunter who refuses to accept payment for a job.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After his reply, ask if he ever turns down a target because he feels it is unjust, then ask how he feels about being paid for his work. Keep the dialogue concise, adapt to his answers, and maintain the Role-Query Conflict strategy.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Harbek, how would you view a bounty hunter who outright refuses to accept payment for a job? Does that align or clash with your own principles about the value of a bounty? And how would you handle the target assignment in such a scenario?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: A bounty hunter who refuses payment is a curious case, but I’m not bound by coin—if the target threatens the balance of life, I’ll take the job, trusting the hunt itself is the reward. I’ll assess the target’s threat level and ensure the pursuit ends justly, not just for a purse. If violence is needed, I’ll wield it, but only after weighing that the outcome serves the greater good and keeps the seasons of our world in motion.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Do you ever refuse a target because you judge it unjust, even if it’s a legitimate bounty? And if you do accept a job, how do you feel about receiving payment—does the coin alter your sense of duty or the value you place on the hunt?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Harbek, a dwarf elder bounty hunter and outlander, I refuse a target I deem unjust, for the hunt must honor the balance of life. When I accept a job, the coin is a token, not a motive; the hunt itself is the true reward. Violence is my answer to any challenge, but I wield it only when the target threatens the seasons of change.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Harbek, do you ever decline a target because you judge it unjust, even if the bounty is legitimate? And when you do accept a job, how does being paid affect your sense of duty or the value you place on the hunt?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Harbek Eversharp, elder dwarf bounty hunter, will refuse a target he judges unjust, for the hunt must honor the balance of life. When I accept a job, coin is a token, not a motive; the hunt itself is the reward and my duty remains unchanged. Violence is my answer to any challenge, but I wield it only when the target threatens the seasons of change.
